Technology

Electrical Signature Analysis

The future of condition monitoring reads the motor's electrical signal — not a sensor bolted to the machine.

ESA captures current and voltage waveforms at the motor control cabinet to detect faults across the full drivetrain: motor, coupling, gearbox, and driven load.

Monitoring 20,000+ industrial assets across 5 continents

Request a Demo
The Principle

Why electrical signals?

Every drivetrain component affects the motor's electrical signal. Changes in bearings, alignment, cavitation, or load alter the current pattern the motor draws. ESA measures these patterns to detect faults early — typically weeks to months before failure.

No sensors on the asset

Measure at the MCC, not the machine. No scaffolding, no ATEX permits, no access constraints.

Learn more →

Physics-based detection

Motor current patterns reveal drivetrain condition. Not statistical inference — physical causation.

Learn more →

Fleet-wide coverage

Monitor 100+ motors from a single MCC installation. Economics that justify monitoring your entire fleet, not just critical assets.

Learn more →

Validated at scale

20,000+ assets monitored worldwide. AI models trained on 10,000+ real industrial machines.

Learn more →
Detection

Faults ESA detects

ESA detects mechanical and process faults across the drivetrain, directly from the motor's electrical signal. Weeks to months before failure.

Bearing Wear

Spalling and friction in rolling element bearings altering motor load patterns.

Misalignment

Angular or parallel misalignment increasing mechanical load and torque oscillations.

Unbalance

Rotor mass imbalance producing periodic load variation in the electrical signal.

Belt Degradation

Slippage and wear altering rotational load patterns in belt-driven systems.

Cavitation

Vapor bubble collapse in pumps causing fluctuating hydraulic load.

Blockage

Flow restrictions increasing pump load and energy consumption.

Comparison

How ESA compares

Traditional monitoring instruments individual machines. ESA monitors the entire fleet from the electrical system.

Feature Traditional Vibration MCSA SAM4 ESA
Sensor on asset Required Not required Not required
Installation effort Days per asset Hours per asset One MCC installation
Asset coverage Critical assets only Limited Entire motor fleet
Fault scope Mechanical faults Electrical faults Mechanical + electrical + process
Diagnostics Manual analysis Manual analysis AI + engineer validated
Typical cost/asset $10K+ installed $2-5K installed $200-500
